role_delineation_check: The Social Worker is responsible for managing the referral process and selecting the home health agency, while the Navigator audits demographic data in Atlantis, monitors for missing updates, and educates the family on the medical assistant's role during the first twenty-four hours at home.
narrative_summary: Mrs. Elena Rodriguez, a 69-year-old patient recovering from a diabetic foot ulcer, faced a potential delay in her transition home when the intake coordinator at the selected home health agency became unavailable. The Navigator noticed that no discharge details had been updated in the Atlantis portal and reached out to the Social Worker, who confirmed the agency was experiencing an administrative bottleneck. By focusing on educating the family about the initial visit window and documenting their concerns about the timeline, the Navigator helped manage expectations during the wait. Once the Social Worker confirmed the agency had processed the referral, the Navigator successfully coordinated the transition, ensuring Mrs. Rodriguez received her first home visit the day after discharge.
demographic_audit_note: I performed a thorough audit of the face sheet against the Atlantis record and discovered that the patient's primary phone number was missing a digit; I updated the portal and notified the Social Worker to ensure the home health agency had the correct contact information.
v_card_flyer_status: I assisted Mrs. Rodriguez in saving the Healing Partners contact information as a V-Card on her smartphone and provided a program flyer to her daughter to ensure they would recognize our calls for the pre-discharge check-in.
pre_dc_pulse_call: I completed the twenty-four-hour pre-discharge call with the patient's daughter, who confirmed that the home was ready for her mother's return and expressed relief that the visit window was clearly defined.
atlantis_final_sync: I recorded the confirmed discharge date and scheduled the first home visit in the Atlantis portal after the Social Worker provided verbal confirmation that the home health agency had finally accepted the case.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The Navigator noticed that no update had been posted to the transition plan in Atlantis for forty-eight hours and checked in with the Social Worker to see if the discharge goal had shifted.
The Social Worker informed the Navigator that the agency intake coordinator was out of the office, which was delaying the official acceptance of the diabetic wound care referral.
The Navigator documented the family's anxiety regarding the lack of a confirmed home health start date in Atlantis and alerted the Social Worker to the need for a family update.
The Social Worker confirmed the agency finally accepted the referral, allowing the Navigator to update the status in Atlantis and finalize the first visit time with the family.

format_2_triples.situation: The Atlantis portal showed the referral status as pending for several days without a confirmed agency nurse.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Asked the Social Worker for a verbal status update to determine if the home health agency had received the clinical orders.

format_2_triples.situation: The family expressed confusion about whether the Navigator would be the one changing the surgical dressings at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the family on the specific role of the medical assistant and deferred all clinical wound care questions to the facility nursing staff.

format_2_triples.situation: The patient's contact information in the system did not match the information provided by the daughter during the initial meeting.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Verified the correct phone number and address during a bedside visit and updated the records in the Atlantis portal.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to provide an update on the agency's response during the next scheduled multidisciplinary meeting.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the boundary of the Social Worker's role in managing vendor communications and prevents the Navigator from interfering with the referral process.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Verbally ask the Social Worker for a status update on the agency's intake process and then inform the family about what to expect during the first twenty-four hours at home.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This allows the Navigator to gather necessary information to support the family's transition while staying within the liaison scope and deferring vendor management to the Social Worker.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the intake department of the home health agency directly to ask why they have not yet assigned a nurse to Mrs. Rodriguez's case.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This violates the no-vendor rule and oversteps into the Social Worker's territory by attempting to manage the agency's internal scheduling and intake operations.
